content: Just as the lexical definition of reflexion encompasses both optical reflection of external light and internal deliberate cognitive reflection, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to integrate two complementary modes: "reflecting" to map inconsistent external feedback from task environments the way a mirror reflects light, and deliberate "reflection" to critique one’s own reasoning outputs. This dual framing unlocks a new structured self-reflection pipeline for LLMs that outperforms single-perspective reflection schemes.
